intro: Roebuckiella lathamensis, also known as Latham's fern or Roebuck's fern, is a type of fern species belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Roebuckiella lathamensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
